mutants.invariant: The configured canonical origin, never Host, decides the browser origin boundary.
A caller-supplied client IP is anonymous unless its private-hop signature verifies.
OAuth privilege callbacks allow only the literal boolean true.
An OAuth resource accepts only the oauth-access token class.
An OAuth access token is bound to the configured audience.
The requested resource must equal the signed access-token audience.
The expected client, client_id, and azp must agree exactly.
Every signed scope is allowlisted and every operation-required scope is present.
Security parameters are singletons; first/last-value parsing is forbidden.
Token requests must use exactly the client's registered confidential Basic or public none authentication profile.
OAuth redirect URIs are exact canonical registrations without wildcards.
Authorization requests require an exact 43-character S256 challenge.
The token exchange redirect URI must equal the URI persisted with the code.
Caller-supplied host, forwarding, IP, and x-bcn controls never cross the proxy.
consumeOne is one component mutation, never a query followed by deletion.
Bulk updates cannot change the logical id.
Bulk updates cannot change a unique field.
